vendors.name: InvoiceQ
vendors.tagline: Dedicated JoFotara e-invoicing platform + ERP/POS connector
vendors.market_position: A specialist e-invoicing platform with a dedicated Jordan portal, built to connect businesses to the National E-Invoicing System (JoFotara). It sits as a compliance layer on top of existing ERP/accounting/POS systems (Dynamics, QuickBooks, Oracle, SAP) rather than replacing them — one of the most visible JoFotara-first vendors in the market.
cons: A compliance layer, not a full accounting/construction system
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: JOD 120/yr (≤200 invoices), JOD 240/yr (≤500), JOD 500/yr (unlimited); full ISTD-integration tier quote-based.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Purpose-built around JoFotara — handles the UUID, QR, XML signing and real-time ISTD transmission, with a custom API-integration tier for firms that want to keep their existing back-office system.

vendors.name: Mozon
vendors.tagline: Amman-built ERP with a dedicated contracting module + JoFotara connectors
vendors.market_position: An Amman-headquartered enterprise-software house (offices also in Cairo and Dubai) offering ERP, accounting, HR and BI. Notably it ships Mozon Contracting Management (MCM) for the construction sector plus pre-built ISTD connectors — one of the few Jordan-native vendors that pairs a real contracting module with national e-invoicing compliance.
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (request a demo); no public JOD price.
vendors.jo_specific_note: The standout for Jordanian contractors specifically — a real contracting module plus "plug-and-play" ISTD/JoFotara connectors (it claims 3–5 day integration), from a local vendor that also offers Arabic support.

vendors.name: Odoo
vendors.tagline: Global open-source ERP with native JoFotara e-invoicing modules
vendors.market_position: The dominant global open-source ERP, uniquely well-supported in Jordan via a native fiscal localization (l10n_jo) plus a dedicated JoFotara module (l10n_jo_edi). Widely deployed locally through partners (e.g. Stride) — the most credible "global tool, fully localized" pick for a contractor who wants project, inventory and accounting in one platform.
pros: Native Jordan localization + JoFotara EDI module
Full ERP: project, inventory, accounting, HR
Arabic + English; scalable from SME up
cons: Needs a partner to implement well
JoFotara needs ISTD API credentials configured
vendors.typical_pricing: Per-app SaaS (USD) on odoo.com; Community edition free/self-hosted; implementation via partner (quote).
vendors.jo_specific_note: Ships the Jordan chart of accounts, 16% sales tax and JoFotara submission (UBL 1.2, QR onto the PDF, verifiable via the Sanad app) out of the box — but you need ISTD API credentials and usually a local partner to implement it.

vendors.name: Daftra
vendors.tagline: Arabic-first MENA accounting & invoicing with a Jordan e-invoice module
vendors.market_position: A widely-used regional MENA accounting/invoicing platform with a dedicated Jordanian e-invoice product and direct ISTD integration. It includes a Real Estate & Construction industry profile with project tracking, making it a workable single tool for smaller Arabic-language contracting firms that want jobs and books together.
pros: Has a Real Estate & Construction profile
cons: Construction depth is light vs a true construction ERP
JO pricing not fixed on the page
vendors.typical_pricing: Tiered SaaS (see daftra.com/en/plans); JOD-denominated invoices; 14-day trial.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Direct JoFotara integration with auto QR and ISTD-compliant formats, JOD-aware and bilingual — its construction support is a general industry profile rather than a deep contracting ERP, so it suits smaller firms.

vendors.name: FawtraPlus
vendors.tagline: Low-cost approved JoFotara invoicing for Jordanian micro-businesses
vendors.market_position: A budget, Jordan-native e-invoicing app (by JoPython) positioned as an approved JoFotara program and an upgrade over the government’s basic app. A strong fit for very small firms and sole traders who need compliant invoicing cheaply, with inventory and reporting on top.
pros: Very cheap (~50 JOD/yr) with a published price
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: 50 JOD/year (~4.17 JOD/month).
vendors.jo_specific_note: The cheapest verified compliant option — it explicitly handles 16% GST + income tax, QR and the tax number, with offline (PWA) and WhatsApp/email delivery, built squarely for the Jordanian micro-business.

vendors.name: Fatwarti
vendors.tagline: Jordanian accounting + POS with direct JoFotara invoicing
vendors.market_position: An Amman-based accounting/POS system with direct, official-API JoFotara integration. It covers sales, purchases, expenses, receivables/payables, inventory and a cashier/POS module — aimed at retail and service SMEs rather than construction specifically.
cons: POS only on the advanced tier
vendors.typical_pricing: Tiered (JOD-denominated, not displayed publicly); free trial.
vendors.jo_specific_note: 100% JoFotara-compatible via the official API and bilingual — usable by a small trades business for compliant books, but it is a retail/service tool, not a contracting system.

vendors.name: Stride (Stride Business Solutions)
vendors.tagline: Amman-based Odoo implementation partner with JoFotara integration
vendors.market_position: A Jordanian (Amman) Odoo implementation and ERP-consulting firm that delivers Odoo as a full business system and handles the JoFotara compliance layer for local clients. Best understood as the local integrator that makes a global ERP (Odoo) work for Jordan, rather than a product vendor of its own.
cons: A services/implementation firm, not a product
No dedicated construction module of its own
Value depends on Odoo licensing on top
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (free consultation); Odoo licensing separate.
vendors.jo_specific_note: The practical route to Odoo in Jordan — it configures the l10n_jo / l10n_jo_edi JoFotara modules and provides Arabic-language support for contractors who want Odoo but need the e-invoicing handled.

vendors.name: Tax2Gov (T2G)
vendors.tagline: Universal JoFotara API connector / middleware for ERPs & POS
vendors.market_position: An e-invoicing middleware provider offering a universal API connector that bridges existing ERP/POS systems to Jordan’s ISTD JoFotara platform, including a documented Odoo plugin. Aimed at higher-volume, multi-industry firms that want to keep their current system and add compliance.
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (free consultation).
vendors.jo_specific_note: A pure JoFotara compliance layer that sits between your ERP and ISTD — useful if you have a system you like and just need the clearance bridge, but confirm local support expectations since it is not Jordan-headquartered.

vendors.name: ESKADENIA Software
vendors.tagline: Established Jordan-native ERP house (finance, SCM, HR, manufacturing)
vendors.market_position: A long-established (2000), CMMI-5, Amman-headquartered Jordanian software company with a broad ERP suite — financials, SCM, HR, manufacturing, fleet. A credible local enterprise vendor, but its ERP is general-purpose: no dedicated construction module, and no JoFotara claim was found on its site.
cons: JoFotara/e-invoicing not confirmed on its site
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Jordan-native and locally supported, but on the public evidence it is neither construction-specific nor confirmed JoFotara-ready — a buyer should confirm e-invoicing handling and project-costing depth directly before relying on it for a contracting workflow.

vendors.name: Zoho Books
vendors.tagline: Affordable global cloud accounting; JoFotara via a third-party connector
vendors.market_position: A popular, affordable global cloud-accounting product used by Jordanian SMEs — but with no native JoFotara support. Jordan compliance is achieved via third-party Marketplace connectors. Workable for small trades firms already on Zoho, provided they add and maintain a connector.
vendors.reputation_flag: No native JoFotara support — relies on a third-party Zoho Marketplace connector for ISTD compliance; verify the connector’s current certification before relying on it.
cons: Connector adds cost + a dependency to maintain
Compliance only as good as the third-party plugin
vendors.typical_pricing: Published per-org tiers (USD) on zoho.com; free tier exists; JoFotara connector priced separately.
vendors.jo_specific_note: JoFotara compliance is bolt-on, not built-in — you must install and pay for a Zoho Marketplace connector to transmit to ISTD, so confirm the connector is currently certified before you commit.

vendors.name: QuickBooks
vendors.tagline: Global SMB accounting; JoFotara only via middleware
vendors.market_position: Globally dominant SMB accounting software with an install base in Jordan, but not JoFotara-compliant on its own. Jordanian users connect it to the National E-Invoicing System through middleware (e.g. InvoiceQ, Tax2Gov). Reasonable for firms already standardized on QuickBooks who layer a connector on top.
vendors.reputation_flag: Not JoFotara-native — requires external middleware (e.g. InvoiceQ, Tax2Gov) to transmit to ISTD.
cons: Localization weaker than Odoo for Jordan
vendors.typical_pricing: Published subscription (USD) on intuit.com; JoFotara middleware priced separately.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Treat QuickBooks as the books and a separate ISTD connector (InvoiceQ, Tax2Gov) as the JoFotara bridge — there is no native Jordan e-invoicing inside QuickBooks itself.

vendors.name: JONEPS
vendors.tagline: Jordan’s official government e-procurement portal — where public tenders are won
vendors.market_position: The national government e-procurement system (launched 2018), run by the Government Tenders Directorate and General Supplies Department. E-tendering adoption reached ~100%, so for any contractor chasing Jordanian public-sector work, registering and bidding here is effectively mandatory infrastructure — the Jordanian analogue to Saudi Etimad.
pros: The official channel for all Jordanian government tenders
cons: Government-portal UX, not a slick lead feed
No notification/intelligence layer of its own
vendors.typical_pricing: Government platform — free to register and bid.
vendors.jo_specific_note: The single channel for government construction tenders in Jordan — you must be JoFotara-registered and JCCA-classified to participate, so it ties directly to the compliance and certification entries here.

vendors.name: JordanTenders
vendors.tagline: Subscription tender-intelligence aggregator covering Jordan (incl. construction)
vendors.market_position: A commercial subscription tender-aggregation service that consolidates Jordanian government, PSU and private tenders into a searchable, filterable feed with alerts and Excel export. "Construction & Real Estate" is a primary category — a convenience/intelligence layer over sources like JONEPS, though it is not Jordan-rooted.
vendors.reputation_flag: Foreign aggregator (Global Tenders Services, India) reselling tender data — verify freshness/coverage against the free official JONEPS portal before subscribing.
pros: Aggregates government + private + NGO tenders in one feed
Alerts + Excel export on paid tiers
cons: Overlaps with free JONEPS for public tenders
vendors.typical_pricing: Paid subscription (tiers not public); free trial.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Useful if you want construction tenders pushed to you (alerts, filters, export) rather than checking JONEPS manually — but for public tenders it largely overlaps the free official portal, so weigh the subscription against going direct.

vendors.name: JOFICO (Jordan French Insurance)
vendors.tagline: Established Jordanian-French insurer with a full engineering line
vendors.market_position: Founded 1976 with a French insurance-group partnership; a mid-to-upper-tier multi-line insurer, ASE-listed, well-regarded for corporate and engineering lines.
pros: Contractors All Risks + engineering named explicitly on its own site
cons: Mid-size balance sheet vs the market leaders
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only via broker/agent.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Its own product page explicitly lists Contractors All Risks (مخاطر المقاولين الشامل), Plant & Machinery, Machinery Breakdown and Erection All Risks — the strongest first-party verification of CAR cover of any Jordanian insurer we found.

vendors.name: Arabia Insurance – Jordan (AICJ)
vendors.tagline: Full engineering-line insurer with English product pages
vendors.market_position: Incorporated 1975, Amman-HQ, ASE-listed — an established multi-line Jordanian insurer with a clearly documented engineering line.
pros: Full CAR + EAR + Plant & Machinery on an English page (rare in JO)
cons: Professional indemnity not confirmed on the engineering page
Brand confusable with the regional Arabia Insurance
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only via broker/agent.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Its English engineering page explicitly lists Contractor’s All Risks, Erection All Risks, Plant & Machinery, Machinery Breakdown and more — useful when you want the cover spelled out in English before talking to a broker.

vendors.name: gig – Jordan (Arab Orient)
vendors.tagline: Market-leading, regionally-backed multi-line insurer
vendors.market_position: Founded 1996 as Arab Orient; joined Gulf Insurance Group (a top-3 MENA insurer) in 2013. Among the largest insurers in Jordan by premium and AM Best-rated — a true market leader with deep reinsurance capacity for large infrastructure.
cons: No fetchable static CAR page (JS-rendered site)
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only via broker/branch.
vendors.jo_specific_note: The GIG backing means the capacity to underwrite large Jordanian infrastructure risk — its engineering line covers CAR, plant & machinery, machinery breakdown and more, placed through a broker rather than online.

vendors.name: Jordan Insurance Company (JIC)
vendors.tagline: Jordan’s oldest insurer (since 1951)
vendors.market_position: Founded 1951 — the oldest insurer in Jordan and among the oldest in the Middle East. ASE-listed, operating in Jordan, the UAE and Kuwait; a long-standing top-tier multi-line insurer and reinsurer.
pros: Oldest and most established Jordanian insurer
CAR + Erection All Risks + Plant & Machinery
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only via broker/agent.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Heritage plus a JO/UAE/KW footprint and a documented engineering line that includes professional indemnity — a solid established option, though you’ll confirm the specifics through an agent.

vendors.name: Middle East Insurance Company (MEICO)
vendors.tagline: Multi-line insurer strong in engineering & marine
vendors.market_position: Founded 1962, Amman-HQ, ASE-listed — reported #1 among Jordanian insurers in shareholders’ equity, marine and engineering. Financially strong and corporate-focused.
cons: Engineering sub-products not enumerated on a loadable page
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only via broker/agent.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Structures its cover into Property, General Accident, Engineering ("risks that arise from contracting and engineering business"), Marine and more — strong on engineering by reputation, though you’ll get the CAR specifics by contacting them.

vendors.name: ZenHR
vendors.tagline: Modern Jordan-origin HR & payroll SaaS — SSC + income tax on-page
vendors.market_position: An Amman-HQ HR/payroll SaaS (founded 2016, venture-backed) — the most visible Jordan-origin HRMS, serving KSA, UAE, Jordan, Egypt, Iraq and Kuwait. The default modern pick for a Jordanian firm.
pros: Native Jordan SSC + income-tax + end-of-service, stated on its own page
Modern bilingual cloud UI + employee self-service
cons: Jordan is one of several markets it serves
A full HRMS may exceed a small contractor’s payroll-only need
SMB-to-enterprise positioning can cost more than a basic local tool
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only.
vendors.jo_specific_note: The only payroll vendor that states Jordan SSC + income-tax handling verbatim on its own Jordan page ("100% compliant with Jordan Labor Law & SSC"), including end-of-service — the cleanest fit if you want confidence on local compliance.

vendors.name: MenaITech / MenaPAY
vendors.tagline: Amman-HQ enterprise HR/payroll incumbent ("hire to retire")
vendors.market_position: An Amman-HQ HR/payroll house (founded 2003) — the region’s legacy enterprise HCM incumbent, with ~2,000 clients across MENA and strong banking-sector adoption. Suite spans MenaHR, MenaPAY, MenaME and MenaTA.
pros: Deep, long-established Jordan vendor with a bank-grade track record
Full HCM suite under one roof
Handles social-security deductions via country profiles
cons: Enterprise-oriented — heavier/pricier than a small contractor needs
Jordan SSC is via "country profiles" — confirm granularity in a demo
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Amman-headquartered with a bank-grade Jordan track record; MenaPAY handles social-security deductions through localized country profiles — confirm the exact Jordan SSC configuration in a demo, as its public page foregrounds Saudi WPS.

vendors.name: Yomly (formerly EmiratesHR)
vendors.tagline: Enterprise HR & payroll with a dedicated Jordan offering
vendors.market_position: A Dubai-HQ enterprise HR/payroll platform (founded 2018, rebranded from EmiratesHR) with ~200+ enterprise clients across 50+ countries and a dedicated Jordan offering.
pros: States Jordan labor/tax alignment + automated deductions
cons: UAE-HQ — Jordan is a secondary market
Does not name "SSC" explicitly — confirm in a demo
Enterprise-scale likely over-budget for a small trades firm
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Its Jordan page says it is "designed to align with local labor laws and tax regulations in Jordan" and automates deductions — but it does not name the SSC by acronym, so verify the Jordan social-security handling before committing.

vendors.name: GTS One ERP
vendors.tagline: Amman-based ERP-integrated HR & payroll
vendors.market_position: An Amman-based ERP/HR-payroll vendor serving Jordan and the GCC, targeting firms that want an Arabic HRMS/payroll tied into a broader ERP, with biometric attendance and bank direct-deposit.
vendors.reputation_flag: Jordan SSC / income-tax automation is not stated on its site — a genuine Amman vendor, but confirm SSC compliance directly before relying on it as a compliant payroll.
pros: Genuinely Amman-based with a Jordan payroll product
ERP-integrated (useful if you want the ERP too)
cons: No explicit SSC/income-tax statement on its site
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only.
vendors.jo_specific_note: A real Amman-based, ERP-integrated payroll option — but its site lists generic features without naming Jordan SSC or income-tax automation, so treat the local-compliance claim as "verify directly".

vendors.name: Jordan Construction Contractors Association (JCCA)
vendors.tagline: The mandatory body + classification that gate public-works bidding
vendors.market_position: The statutory contractor union (founded 1972) under Construction Contractors Law No. 13/1987. Working membership is a legal prerequisite to be licensed and classified; classification itself is administered by the Government Tenders Directorate (MPWH) under Govt Works Regulation No. 71/1986, and the certificate gates public-tender bidding.
pros: The single mandatory credential that unlocks public-works bidding
Ties contractors into the recognized professional body
cons: Multi-agency sequence (JCCA + CCD + license + classification) — slow
Certificate expires every 3 years; renewal needs updated audited financials
Required grade is value-gated — bid below your grade and you’re excluded
vendors.typical_pricing: Membership dues + per-grade classification fees (see official schedule); certificate valid 3 years.
vendors.jo_specific_note: The load-bearing credential — you must be a JCCA working member and hold a classification certificate to bid public tenders, and the required grade scales with project value (grades run from a 6th entry tier up to "first grade"). Renewal every 3 years needs updated financials, owned-equipment evidence and a qualifying resident engineer.

vendors.name: Jordan Engineers Association (JEA)
vendors.tagline: Mandatory registration to practice engineering or run an engineering office
vendors.market_position: The statutory body (society 1948, licensed 1949; 143,000+ members) governing the engineering profession — admission, registration, certificates and standards. Projects depend on JEA-registered engineers to design and sign off work.
pros: Legally required to practice engineering or run an engineering office
A registered engineer satisfies the contractor-classification "resident engineer" condition
Issues a recognized certificate + ID to stamp work
cons: Foreign / 3-year-degree holders face extra academic conditions
Fees/procedures not published as a clear schedule
vendors.typical_pricing: Admission fee + annual dues (see official schedule); office registration separate.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Per its governing law, no one may practice engineering in Jordan without JEA registration — and because a JEA-registered engineer also satisfies the JCCA classification "resident engineer" requirement, it is a direct dependency of being able to bid public works.

vendors.name: Companies Control Department (CCD)
vendors.tagline: Jordan’s company registrar — the first legal step to operate
vendors.market_position: The national company registrar under the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Supply, administering Companies Law No. 22/1997 — registering LLCs, PSCs, partnerships and foreign branches, plus amendments, filings and beneficial-ownership/AML.
pros: Establishes the legal entity — prerequisite for licensing, tax + JCCA classification
cons: Only one link in a longer chain
vendors.typical_pricing: Fees scale by company type + capital (see official schedule); e-services online.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Company registration here is the foundational step and an explicit prerequisite in the classification chain (registration precedes JCCA classification) — the start of the CCD → ISTD → license → JCCA → classification → SSC sequence.

vendors.name: Income and Sales Tax Department (ISTD)
vendors.tagline: Jordan’s tax authority — income tax, 16% GST, and JoFotara e-invoicing
vendors.market_position: Formed 2004 under the Ministry of Finance; it runs income/sales-tax registration, returns and payment, and the JoFotara national e-invoicing system (~97% of income returns are e-filed; payments via eFawateercom).
pros: Establishes the tax file — required to operate, invoice and bid
cons: GST registration is threshold-triggered — easy to miss as revenue grows
JoFotara e-invoicing is now mandatory (a systems requirement)
vendors.typical_pricing: No registration fee; the obligation is the tax itself (GST 16%; e-filing via portal/eFawateercom).
vendors.jo_specific_note: The same authority that runs the mandatory JoFotara clearance platform (live since 1 April 2025) — your tax file feeds everything, and any invoice not cleared through JoFotara is invalid for VAT input deduction, so the software and the tax registration are now inseparable.

vendors.name: Social Security Corporation (SSC)
vendors.tagline: Mandatory social insurance — register from the first employee
vendors.market_position: The statutory social-insurance institution — old-age pensions, work-injury, disability, death, maternity and unemployment — funded by employer + employee contributions. The mandatory employment-compliance backbone.
pros: Mandatory employer compliance to legally employ workers
cons: Mandatory from the first employee — no small-employer exemption
~21.75% wage-based cost is material and recurring
Foreign workers must subscribe before a work permit is issued
vendors.typical_pricing: No registration fee; ongoing ~21.75% of insured wage (employer 14.25% + employee 7.5%); 1%/month late interest.
vendors.jo_specific_note: Participation is mandatory for every establishment with at least one worker, at ~21.75% of the insured wage paid within 15 days of month-end — which is exactly why Jordanian payroll tools (ZenHR, MenaPAY) lead with SSC handling.
